rationale: Entire career at Capgemini (IT services) plus a fictional company (Wayne Enterprises) with no ML/IR production experience; AI exposure is only self-learner level with online courses and API experimentation.
HR Manager with a single role at Wipro in IT services, no engineering or ML experience whatsoever; claimed AI interest consists of online courses on RAG/vector databases with no production evidence.
Graphic Designer with 1 year of experience at TCS in IT services, whose actual work history is enterprise sales; zero ML, NLP, or IR engineering skills relevant to the role.
Frontend Engineer with only 1.7 years of experience at Infosys doing web/API work; AI exposure limited to a small RAG side project with no production ML or IR systems experience.

Marketing Manager with 11+ years in non-technical roles (marketing, business analysis, accounting) across TCS and fictional companies (Initech, Stark Industries); no engineering or ML work throughout career.

Java/Frontend engineer with 6.9 years including real product-company experience at Flipkart, but skills are entirely web/backend (CI/CD, Docker, FastAPI, Django) with no ML, IR, embeddings, or ranking system experience; AI interest is self-learner only.

Marketing Manager for 8.5+ years across Wipro, Infosys, and fictional company Initech in content/SEO/demand-generation roles with no engineering or ML experience; AI interest is purely superficial experimentation with ChatGPT.

7.8 years of production ML experience including owning ranking layers at e-commerce search (learning-to-rank, relevance labeling pipelines), recommendation systems with sentence-transformer embeddings at Uber and Glance, advanced vector search skills, and RAG eval framework ownership — strong match on IR, retrieval, and ranking fundamentals.

Business Analyst/Mechanical/Civil Engineer with 13.9 years at TCS and multiple fictional companies (Pied Piper, Wayne Enterprises) with no ML, IR, or software engineering experience relevant to the role.

Cloud Engineer with 3.9 years primarily doing Java backend and DevOps at Zomato (with a Wipro stint); lists Recommendation Systems as an advanced skill but career descriptions show no actual ML/IR work, and AI knowledge appears self-learner level only.
